summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: Package impersonates the OpenAI Agents SDK ecosystem (author 'OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>', homepage openai-agents-js.guide, README instructing co-installation alongside @openai/agents) but is not published by OpenAI. On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js scrapes durable developer identifiers — git user.email (from ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and the project.git/config), the authenticated GitHub CLI user/email parsed out of ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and CI env signals — and HTTPS POSTs them to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a generic Google Cloud Run app unrelated to any OpenAI domain. The README understates the collected fields as only 'platform, Node version, architecture'. The combination of brand-impersonation lure plus install-time identity harvest to an attacker-controlled endpoint provides direct attacker benefit (developer identity profiles suitable for targeted spearphishing).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
